Title: Create new page in Page Builder
Preconditions: Admin logged in; CMS module installed
Steps:
1. Navigate to Content > Pages
2. Click Add button
3. Enter page name and permalink
4. Select language
5. Click Save
Expected Result: New page created with Draft status; appears in page list

Title: Edit created page
Steps:
1. Open existing page from list
2. Edit Name and Permalink fields
3. Click Save
Expected Result: Page updated successfully with new values

Title: Preview page
Steps:
1. Open page
2. Click Preview
Expected Result: Page preview displayed correctly

Title: Open page in designer (Builder)
Steps:
1. Open page
2. Click Open Designer button
Expected Result: Builder/Designer opens for page editing

Title: Archive page
Preconditions: Page exists in Draft or Published status
Steps:
1. Open page
2. Click Archive button
3. Confirm
Expected Result: Page status changes to Archived

Title: Publish draft page
Steps:
1. Open draft page
2. Click Publish button
Expected Result: Page status transitions from Draft to Published

Title: Publish to Archive transition
Steps:
1. Open published page
2. Click Archive
Expected Result: Page status transitions from Published to Archived

Title: Draft to Archive transition
Steps:
1. Open draft page
2. Click Archive
Expected Result: Page status transitions from Draft to Archived

Title: Verify Draft status badge in grid
Steps:
1. Navigate to Pages list
2. Locate draft page in grid
Expected Result: Draft badge displayed with correct color styling

Title: Verify Published status badge in grid
Steps:
1. Navigate to Pages list
2. Locate published page
Expected Result: Published badge displayed with correct color styling

Title: Verify Archived status badge in grid
Steps:
1. Navigate to Pages list
2. Locate archived page
Expected Result: Archived badge displayed with correct color styling

Title: Verify Scheduled badge in grid
Steps:
1. Navigate to Pages list
2. Locate scheduled page
Expected Result: Scheduled badge displayed alongside status badge

Title: Verify Personalized badge in grid
Preconditions: Page with visibility/user group restrictions exists
Steps:
1. Navigate to Pages list
2. Locate personalized page
Expected Result: Personalized badge displayed alongside status badge

Title: Grid columns display correctly
Steps:
1. Navigate to Pages list
2. Verify all columns visible
Expected Result: Grid displays name, status, badges, dates correctly

Title: Checkbox selection and bulk selection
Steps:
1. Select individual page checkboxes
2. Use bulk select checkbox
Expected Result: Individual and bulk selection works; totals counter updates

Title: Search functionality in page list
Steps:
1. Enter search term in search field
2. Verify results
Expected Result: Page list filtered by search term

Title: Status filter - Draft pages only
Steps:
1. Click Draft filter in navigation
2. Verify counter and list
Expected Result: Only draft pages shown; counter matches list count

Title: Status filter - Active (Published) pages only
Steps:
1. Click Active filter in navigation
2. Verify counter and list
Expected Result: Only published/active pages shown; counter matches

Title: Status filter - Pending (Scheduled) pages only
Steps: 1. Click Pending filter in navigation
Expected Result: Only scheduled pages shown; counter matches

Title: Status filter - Archived pages only
Steps: 1. Click Archived filter in navigation
Expected Result: Only archived pages shown; counter matches

Title: Counters update when page status changes
Steps:
1. Note current counter values
2. Publish a draft page
3. Check counters
Expected Result: Draft counter decrements; Active counter increments

Title: Page blade - Basic information fields
Steps:
1. Open a page
2. Verify Basic information section
Expected Result: Name, Permalink (with read-only domain), Language dropdown visible

Title: Scheduling section - set start and end dates
Steps:
1. Open page
2. Expand Scheduling section
3. Set Start date via datepicker
4. Set End date via datepicker
5. Save
Expected Result: Scheduling dates saved; Scheduled badge appears

Title: Personalization - visibility toggle

1. Open page
2. Expand Personalization section
3. Toggle Visibility OFF
4. Save
Expected Result: Visibility setting saved; page restricted to registered users only

Title: Personalization - User groups restriction

3. Select User groups from dropdown

Title: Personalization - Organization restriction

3. Select Organization from dropdown
4. Save
Expected Result: Organization restriction saved; page accessible only to authorized org users

Title: Page builder field types - text and rich text
Steps:

Title: Rich Text Field Type — Add Rich Text Content Block
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Page exists in Page Builder designer
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/pages and open an existing page
2. Click Open Designer to enter the page builder
3. Add a new section or open an existing section for editing
4. Locate the Rich Text field type in the field list
5. Click on the Rich Text field to open the rich text editor
6. Enter formatted text content: bold, italic, and a hyperlink
7. Click Save or apply the changes
8. Preview the page and verify rich text renders correctly
Expected Result:
- Rich Text field type is available in the page builder field list
- Rich text editor opens with full formatting toolbar: bold, italic, underline, links, lists
- Formatted content is saved and persisted correctly
- Page preview renders the rich text with all applied formatting styles
- HTML output is clean and valid without extraneous markup

Title: Form Field Type (Draft) — Create Form Field in Page
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Page exists in Page Builder designer
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/pages and open an existing page
2. Click Open Designer to enter the page builder
3. Add a new section for editing
4. Locate the Form field type in the field list
5. Click on the Form field to open its configuration
6. Enter a field label and placeholder text
7. Set the field as required if the option is available
8. Save and preview the page
9. Verify the form field renders in draft state on the page
Expected Result:
- Form field type is available in the page builder section
- Configuration panel opens for the form field with label and placeholder options
- Form field is saved with the configured settings
- Page preview shows the form field in the correct position
- Draft form field is displayed but may not be fully functional until published

Title: Select Field Type — Add Dropdown Select Field
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Page exists in Page Builder designer
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/pages and open an existing page
2. Click Open Designer to enter the page builder
3. Add a new section or open an existing section
4. Locate the Select/Dropdown field type in the field list
5. Click on the Select field to open its configuration
6. Add multiple options (e.g., Option A, Option B, Option C)
7. Set a default selected option
8. Save and preview the page
9. Verify the dropdown renders with all configured options
Expected Result:
- Select field type is available in the page builder
- Configuration allows adding multiple selectable options
- Default option can be designated
- All configured options appear in the dropdown on the page preview
- Select field maintains its configuration after saving and reopening
- Dropdown styling is consistent with the page theme

Title: Page Section — Image with Text
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Page exists in Page Builder designer
Image file available for upload
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/pages and open a page in the designer
2. Click Add Section
3. Select the Image with Text section type
4. Upload an image in the image field
5. Enter text content in the text area
6. Configure image position (left or right of text)
7. Save the section
8. Preview the page and verify the layout
Expected Result:
- Image with Text section is available in the section type picker
- Image upload accepts common image formats (JPG, PNG, WebP)
- Text and image render side by side as configured
- Image position toggle works correctly (left/right alignment)
- Section is saved and persists after page reload
- Responsive layout adapts correctly on mobile viewport preview

Title: Page Section — Text Over Image
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Page exists in Page Builder designer
Image file available for upload
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/pages and open a page in the designer
2. Click Add Section
3. Select the Text Over Image section type
4. Upload a background image
5. Enter overlay text content
6. Configure text position and color for contrast
7. Save the section
8. Preview the page and verify text overlays the image correctly
Expected Result:
- Text Over Image section is available in the section type picker
- Background image is uploaded and displayed behind the text overlay
- Text renders on top of the image with correct positioning
- Text color and styling provide readable contrast against the background image
- Section renders correctly in page preview with proper z-index layering
- Image dimensions are maintained without distortion

Title: Page Section — Cards List
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Page exists in Page Builder designer
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/pages and open a page in the designer
2. Click Add Section
3. Select the Cards List section type
4. Add at least 3 card items
5. For each card: enter title, description, and optional image
6. Configure the card layout (grid columns)
7. Save the section
8. Preview the page and verify cards display correctly
Expected Result:
- Cards List section is available in the section type picker
- Multiple card items can be added with title, description, and image
- Cards are displayed in a grid layout as configured
- Each card renders with the correct content and styling
- Adding, reordering, and removing cards works correctly
- Cards list is responsive and adjusts on different viewport sizes

Title: Page Section — Simple Text Block
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Page exists in Page Builder designer
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/pages and open a page in the designer
2. Click Add Section
3. Select the Simple Text Block section type
4. Enter plain text content in the text area
5. Configure text alignment and font settings if available
6. Save the section
7. Preview the page and verify text displays correctly
8. Verify the section does not include any unexpected HTML tags
Expected Result:
- Simple Text Block section is available in the section type picker
- Plain text content is entered and saved correctly
- Text renders on the page preview with configured alignment
- No rich text formatting is applied unless explicitly configured
- Section content is clean and readable in the page output
- Section can be edited after creation to update text content

Title: Page Section — Two Column Text
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Page exists in Page Builder designer
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/pages and open a page in the designer
2. Click Add Section
3. Select the Two Column Text section type
4. Enter text content in the left column
5. Enter different text content in the right column
6. Configure column proportions if available
7. Save the section
8. Preview the page and verify both columns display correctly side by side
Expected Result:
- Two Column Text section is available in the section type picker
- Both left and right columns accept and save text content independently

- Column content is editable independently without affecting the other column

Title: Page Section — Simple Image
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Page exists in Page Builder designer
Image file available for upload
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/pages and open a page in the designer
2. Click Add Section
3. Select the Simple Image section type
4. Upload an image file using the image upload field
5. Add optional alt text for accessibility
6. Configure image alignment (center, left, right)
7. Save the section
8. Preview the page and verify the image displays correctly
Expected Result:

- Image upload accepts JPEG, PNG, and WebP formats
- Uploaded image renders at appropriate size without distortion

Title: Navigation Menu Counter Display
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Multiple pages exist in various statuses (Draft, Published, Archived)
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/pages
2. Observe the navigation menu or sidebar with page status filters
3. Check the counter displayed next to each status category (Draft, Active, Pending, Archived)
4. Compare the counter numbers against the actual page count in each category
5. Create a new draft page and verify the Draft counter increments
6. Publish a page and verify the Draft counter decrements and Active counter increments
7. Verify total count updates dynamically without page refresh
Expected Result:
- Counter badges are visible next to each status category in the navigation menu
- Counter numbers accurately reflect the current count of pages in each status
- Creating a new draft page increments the Draft counter by 1
- Publishing a draft page decrements Draft counter and increments Active counter
- Counters update in real time or after status change actions
- Zero counts display as 0 or are hidden depending on UI design

Title: Navigation Menu Filtering — Filter Pages in Nav Menu Blade
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Pages exist in Draft, Published, Pending, and Archived states
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/pages
2. Click on the Draft filter in the navigation menu
3. Verify only draft pages appear in the main list area
4. Click on the Active filter
5. Verify only published/active pages appear
6. Click on the Pending filter
7. Verify only scheduled/pending pages appear
8. Click on the Archived filter
9. Verify only archived pages appear
10. Verify the selected filter is visually highlighted in the menu
Expected Result:
- Each navigation filter correctly limits the page list to the corresponding status
- Draft filter shows only pages with Draft status
- Active filter shows only pages with Published status
- Pending filter shows only pages with Scheduled status
- Archived filter shows only pages with Archived status
- Active filter is visually indicated (bold, highlight, or underline)
- Switching filters updates the list immediately without full page reload

Title: Page Blade — Basic Info Section
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Page exists in the system
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/pages
2. Open an existing page to view its details blade
3. Locate the Basic Information section
4. Verify all fields are present: Name, Permalink, Language
5. Verify the Permalink field shows the read-only domain prefix and editable slug
6. Edit the Name field with a new value
7. Edit the Permalink slug
8. Change the Language dropdown selection
9. Save and verify all changes persist
Expected Result:
- Basic Information section is visible and contains all required fields
- Name field is editable and accepts text input
- Permalink field displays read-only domain URL prefix with editable slug portion
- Language dropdown lists all configured store languages
- All three fields save correctly and persist after page reload
- Permalink updates are reflected in the frontend URL immediately after publishing

Title: Page Blade — Scheduling Section
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Page exists in the system
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/pages and open an existing page
2. Expand the Scheduling section in the page blade
3. Set a future Publish Date using the date picker
4. Set a future Unpublish Date (after the publish date)
5. Save the scheduling settings
6. Verify the Scheduled badge appears in the page list
7. Verify the page is not accessible on the storefront before the publish date
8. Update the publish date to the past and verify the page becomes accessible
9. Verify setting unpublish date in the past removes the page from the storefront
Expected Result:
- Scheduling section is visible and expandable in the page details blade
- Publish Date accepts future date and time via date picker
- Unpublish Date accepts future date and time after the publish date
- Saving scheduling dates causes the Scheduled badge to appear in the grid
- Page follows the schedule: not accessible before publish date, accessible within range
- Setting unpublish date in the past removes the page from storefront
- Validation prevents setting Unpublish Date before Publish Date

Title: Page Blade — Personalization Section
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
User groups and organizations are configured
Page exists in the system
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/pages and open an existing page
2. Expand the Personalization section in the page blade
3. Toggle the Visibility setting to restrict to registered users only
4. Save and verify on storefront that anonymous users cannot access the page
5. Return to admin and select User Groups from the restriction dropdown
6. Select one or more user groups
7. Save and verify the Personalized badge appears in the page list
8. Verify only users belonging to the selected groups can access the page
9. Set Organization restriction and verify organization-level access control
Expected Result:
- Personalization section is visible and expandable in the page details blade
- Visibility toggle restricts page access to authenticated users only when enabled
- User Groups dropdown allows selecting one or more groups for targeted access
- Personalized badge appears in the grid list when any personalization is configured
- Users not in the restricted groups cannot access the page on the storefront
- Organization restriction limits page visibility to members of specified organizations
- Multiple personalization rules can be combined and saved simultaneously

Title: Page Blade — SEO Section
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Page exists in the system
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/pages and open an existing page
2. Expand or navigate to the SEO section in the page blade
3. Enter a Meta Title in the SEO title field
4. Enter a Meta Description in the description field
5. Enter Meta Keywords
6. Save all SEO settings
7. Publish the page if not already published
8. Open the page on the storefront
9. Inspect the page HTML source to verify meta tags are present
Expected Result:
- SEO section is accessible in the page blade with title, description, and keywords fields
- Meta Title, Meta Description, and Keywords fields accept text input and save correctly
- Published page HTML contains the correct meta title tag in the head section
- Meta description is rendered as a meta description tag in the page head
- SEO fields persist after page save and are visible upon reopening the page blade
- Empty SEO fields do not generate invalid or empty meta tags in page output

Title: Page Sorting in List — Sort Pages by Name, Date, and Status
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
At least 5 pages exist with different names and creation dates
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/pages and view the full page list
2. Click the Name column header to sort pages alphabetically A-Z
3. Click the Name column header again to sort Z-A
4. Click the Creation Date column header to sort by oldest first
5. Click the Creation Date column header again to sort by newest first
6. Click the Status column header if available to sort by status
7. Verify the sort order is visually indicated by an arrow icon on the column header
8. Verify the page list reorders correctly after each sort action
Expected Result:
- Clicking column headers sorts the page list by that column
- First click sorts ascending, second click sorts descending
- Name sorting arranges pages in correct alphabetical order
- Date sorting arranges pages by creation date in correct chronological order
- Status sorting groups pages by their current status
- Active sort direction is visually indicated by an arrow or icon on the column header
- Sort state persists when switching between navigation filters

Title: Page Bulk Operations — Select Multiple and Bulk Publish/Unpublish
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
At least 3 draft pages exist
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/pages and filter to show Draft pages
2. Select multiple pages using the individual checkboxes
3. Verify the bulk action toolbar appears with action options
4. Click Bulk Publish
5. Confirm the bulk publish action
6. Verify all selected pages transition to Published status
7. Select multiple Published pages
8. Click Bulk Unpublish or Archive
9. Verify all selected pages transition to the new status
10. Verify the page count counters update accordingly
Expected Result:
- Individual checkboxes and bulk select checkbox are functional in the page list
- Selecting multiple pages activates the bulk action toolbar
- Bulk Publish transitions all selected draft pages to Published status simultaneously
- Bulk Unpublish or Archive transitions all selected pages to the new status
- Page list and status counters update after bulk operations complete
- Confirmation dialog appears before executing destructive bulk operations
- Partial failure is handled gracefully with error feedback for specific pages

Title: Archive Page Workflow — Archive and Verify Storefront Removal
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
A published page exists and is accessible on the storefront
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/pages and identify a currently published page
2. Note the page permalink URL
3. Verify the page is accessible on the storefront using the permalink URL
4. Open the page in the admin and click Archive
5. Confirm the archive action in the dialog
6. Verify the page status changes to Archived in the admin
7. Attempt to access the same permalink URL on the storefront
8. Verify the page is no longer accessible (404 or redirect)
9. Verify the page is listed under the Archived filter in admin
Expected Result:
- Archive action is available for Published pages
- Confirmation dialog appears before archiving
- Page status transitions from Published to Archived after confirmation
- Archived page is no longer accessible on the storefront (returns 404 or redirect)
- Page permalink URL is freed and the page is removed from storefront navigation
- Archived page is visible in the admin under the Archived status filter
- Archived page does not appear in storefront search results

Title: Restore Archived Page — Restore from Archive
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
An archived page exists [CMS-073]
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/pages and click the Archived filter
2. Locate the previously archived page
3. Open the archived page details blade
4. Verify the available actions (Restore or Publish buttons)
5. Click Restore or Publish to restore the page
6. Confirm the action if a dialog appears
7. Verify the page transitions to Draft or Published status
8. Verify the restored page is accessible on the storefront
9. Verify the page appears in the correct status filter in the list
Expected Result:
- Archived pages are accessible in the admin via the Archived status filter
- Restore or Publish action is available on archived page details blade
- Restoring an archived page transitions its status to Draft or Published
- Restored page becomes accessible on the storefront once published
- Page permalink is restored to its original URL
- Page appears correctly in the page list under its new status filter after restore

Title: Multilanguage Page Management — Create Page in Multiple Languages
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Store configured with at least two languages (e.g., English and French)
CMS module installed
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/pages
2. Click Add to create a new page
3. Enter page name and permalink in English
4. Select English as the language
5. Save and publish the English page
6. Verify the English page is accessible on the storefront at the English URL prefix
7. Create another page with the same permalink but select French as the language
8. Add French content to the page and save
9. Publish the French page
10. Verify the French page is accessible at the French language URL prefix
11. Verify the English page at the English URL returns English content
Expected Result:
- Page creation allows selecting a specific language from the configured store languages
- English and French (or configured language) pages can be created with the same permalink
- Each language page is treated as a separate entity in the page list
- English page is accessible at the English storefront URL prefix (e.g., /en/permalink)
- French page is accessible at the French storefront URL prefix (e.g., /fr/permalink)
- Language-specific pages show the correct language badge in the admin list
- Wrong language URL prefix returns 404 for the correct-language-only page
